speaker: KING
text: My blood hath been too cold and temperate,

speaker: KING
text: Unapt to stir at these indignities,

speaker: KING
text: And you have found me, for accordingly

speaker: KING
text: You tread upon my patience. But be sure

speaker: KING
text: I will from henceforth rather be myself,

speaker: KING
text: Mighty and to be feared, than my condition,

speaker: KING
text: Which hath been smooth as oil, soft as young down,

speaker: KING
text: And therefore lost that title of respect

speaker: KING
text: Which the proud soul ne’er pays but to the proud.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Our house, my sovereign liege, little deserves

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The scourge of greatness to be used on it,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And that same greatness too which our own hands

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Have holp to make so portly.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My lord—

speaker: KING
text: Worcester, get thee gone, for I do see

speaker: KING
text: Danger and disobedience in thine eye.

speaker: KING
text: O sir, your presence is too bold and peremptory,

speaker: KING
text: And majesty might never yet endure

speaker: KING
text: The moody frontier of a servant brow.

speaker: KING
text: You have good leave to leave us. When we need

speaker: KING
text: Your use and counsel, we shall send for you.

speaker: KING
text: You were about to speak.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yea, my good lord.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Those prisoners in your Highness’ name demanded,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Which Harry Percy here at Holmedon took,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Were, as he says, not with such strength denied

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: As is delivered to your Majesty.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Either envy, therefore, or misprision

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Is guilty of this fault, and not my son.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My liege, I did deny no prisoners.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But I remember, when the fight was done,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: When I was dry with rage and extreme toil,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Breathless and faint, leaning upon my sword,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Came there a certain lord, neat and trimly dressed,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Fresh as a bridegroom, and his chin new reaped

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Showed like a stubble land at harvest home.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He was perfumèd like a milliner,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And ’twixt his finger and his thumb he held

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A pouncet box, which ever and anon

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He gave his nose and took ’t away again,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Who therewith angry, when it next came there,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Took it in snuff; and still he smiled and talked.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And as the soldiers bore dead bodies by,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He called them untaught knaves, unmannerly,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To bring a slovenly unhandsome corse

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Betwixt the wind and his nobility.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: With many holiday and lady terms

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He questioned me, amongst the rest demanded

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My prisoners in your Majesty’s behalf.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I then, all smarting with my wounds being cold,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To be so pestered with a popinjay,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Out of my grief and my impatience

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Answered neglectingly I know not what—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He should, or he should not; for he made me mad

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To see him shine so brisk and smell so sweet

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And talk so like a waiting-gentlewoman

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of guns, and drums, and wounds—God save the mark!—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And telling me the sovereignest thing on Earth

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Was parmacety for an inward bruise,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And that it was great pity, so it was,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This villainous saltpeter should be digged

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Out of the bowels of the harmless Earth,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Which many a good tall fellow had destroyed

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: So cowardly, and but for these vile guns

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He would himself have been a soldier.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This bald unjointed chat of his, my lord,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I answered indirectly, as I said,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And I beseech you, let not his report

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come current for an accusation

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Betwixt my love and your high Majesty.

speaker: BLUNT
text: The circumstance considered, good my lord,

speaker: BLUNT
text: Whate’er Lord Harry Percy then had said

speaker: BLUNT
text: To such a person and in such a place,

speaker: BLUNT
text: At such a time, with all the rest retold,

speaker: BLUNT
text: May reasonably die and never rise

speaker: BLUNT
text: To do him wrong or any way impeach

speaker: BLUNT
text: What then he said, so he unsay it now.

speaker: KING
text: Why, yet he doth deny his prisoners,

speaker: KING
text: But with proviso and exception

speaker: KING
text: That we at our own charge shall ransom straight

speaker: KING
text: His brother-in-law, the foolish Mortimer,

speaker: KING
text: Who, on my soul, hath willfully betrayed

speaker: KING
text: The lives of those that he did lead to fight

speaker: KING
text: Against that great magician, damned Glendower,

speaker: KING
text: Whose daughter, as we hear, that Earl of March

speaker: KING
text: Hath lately married. Shall our coffers then

speaker: KING
text: Be emptied to redeem a traitor home?

speaker: KING
text: Shall we buy treason and indent with fears

speaker: KING
text: When they have lost and forfeited themselves?

speaker: KING
text: No, on the barren mountains let him starve,

speaker: KING
text: For I shall never hold that man my friend

speaker: KING
text: Whose tongue shall ask me for one penny cost

speaker: KING
text: To ransom home revolted Mortimer.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Revolted Mortimer!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He never did fall off, my sovereign liege,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But by the chance of war. To prove that true

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Needs no more but one tongue for all those wounds,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Those mouthèd wounds, which valiantly he took

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: When on the gentle Severn’s sedgy bank

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In single opposition hand to hand

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He did confound the best part of an hour

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In changing hardiment with great Glendower.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Three times they breathed, and three times did they drink,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Upon agreement, of swift Severn’s flood,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Who then, affrighted with their bloody looks,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Ran fearfully among the trembling reeds

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And hid his crisp head in the hollow bank,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Blood-stainèd with these valiant combatants.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Never did bare and rotten policy

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Color her working with such deadly wounds,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nor never could the noble Mortimer

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Receive so many, and all willingly.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Then let not him be slandered with revolt.

speaker: KING
text: Thou dost belie him, Percy; thou dost belie him.

speaker: KING
text: He never did encounter with Glendower.

speaker: KING
text: I tell thee, he durst as well have met the devil alone

speaker: KING
text: As Owen Glendower for an enemy.

speaker: KING
text: Art thou not ashamed? But, sirrah, henceforth

speaker: KING
text: Let me not hear you speak of Mortimer.

speaker: KING
text: Send me your prisoners with the speediest means,

speaker: KING
text: Or you shall hear in such a kind from me

speaker: KING
text: As will displease you.—My lord Northumberland,

speaker: KING
text: We license your departure with your son.—

speaker: KING
text: Send us your prisoners, or you will hear of it.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: An if the devil come and roar for them,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I will not send them. I will after straight

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And tell him so, for I will ease my heart,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Albeit I make a hazard of my head.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: What, drunk with choler? Stay and pause awhile.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Here comes your uncle.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Speak of Mortimer?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Zounds, I will speak of him, and let my soul

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Want mercy if I do not join with him.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Yea, on his part I’ll empty all these veins

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And shed my dear blood drop by drop in the dust,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But I will lift the downtrod Mortimer

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As high in the air as this unthankful king,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As this ingrate and cankered Bolingbroke.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Brother, the King hath made your nephew mad.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Who struck this heat up after I was gone?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He will forsooth have all my prisoners,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And when I urged the ransom once again

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of my wife’s brother, then his cheek looked pale,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And on my face he turned an eye of death,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Trembling even at the name of Mortimer.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I cannot blame him. Was not he proclaimed

speaker: WORCESTER
text: By Richard, that dead is, the next of blood?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: He was; I heard the proclamation.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And then it was when the unhappy king—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Whose wrongs in us God pardon!—did set forth

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Upon his Irish expedition;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: From whence he, intercepted, did return

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To be deposed and shortly murderèd.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And for whose death we in the world’s wide mouth

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Live scandalized and foully spoken of.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But soft, I pray you. Did King Richard then

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Proclaim my brother Edmund Mortimer

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Heir to the crown?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: He did; myself did hear it.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nay then, I cannot blame his cousin king

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That wished him on the barren mountains starve.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But shall it be that you that set the crown

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Upon the head of this forgetful man

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And for his sake wear the detested blot

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of murderous subornation—shall it be

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That you a world of curses undergo,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Being the agents or base second means,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The cords, the ladder, or the hangman rather?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, pardon me that I descend so low

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To show the line and the predicament

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Wherein you range under this subtle king.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Shall it for shame be spoken in these days,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Or fill up chronicles in time to come,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That men of your nobility and power

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Did gage them both in an unjust behalf

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: (As both of you, God pardon it, have done)

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To put down Richard, that sweet lovely rose,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And plant this thorn, this canker, Bolingbroke?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And shall it in more shame be further spoken

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That you are fooled, discarded, and shook off

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By him for whom these shames you underwent?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No, yet time serves wherein you may redeem

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Your banished honors and restore yourselves

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Into the good thoughts of the world again,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Revenge the jeering and disdained contempt

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of this proud king, who studies day and night

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To answer all the debt he owes to you

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Even with the bloody payment of your deaths.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Therefore I say—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Peace, cousin, say no more.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And now I will unclasp a secret book,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And to your quick-conceiving discontents

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I’ll read you matter deep and dangerous,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As full of peril and adventurous spirit

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As to o’erwalk a current roaring loud

speaker: WORCESTER
text: On the unsteadfast footing of a spear.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If he fall in, good night, or sink or swim!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Send danger from the east unto the west,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: So honor cross it from the north to south,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And let them grapple. O, the blood more stirs

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To rouse a lion than to start a hare!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Imagination of some great exploit

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Drives him beyond the bounds of patience.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By heaven, methinks it were an easy leap

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To pluck bright honor from the pale-faced moon,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Or dive into the bottom of the deep,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Where fathom line could never touch the ground,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And pluck up drownèd honor by the locks,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: So he that doth redeem her thence might wear

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Without corrival all her dignities.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But out upon this half-faced fellowship!

speaker: WORCESTER
text: He apprehends a world of figures here,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: But not the form of what he should attend.—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Good cousin, give me audience for a while.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I cry you mercy.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Those same noble Scots

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That are your prisoners—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll keep them all.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By God, he shall not have a Scot of them.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No, if a Scot would save his soul, he shall not.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll keep them, by this hand!

speaker: WORCESTER
text: You start away

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And lend no ear unto my purposes:

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Those prisoners you shall keep—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nay, I will. That’s flat!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He said he would not ransom Mortimer,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Forbade my tongue to speak of Mortimer.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But I will find him when he lies asleep,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And in his ear I’ll hollo “Mortimer.”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nay, I’ll have a starling shall be taught to speak

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nothing but “Mortimer,” and give it him

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To keep his anger still in motion.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Hear you, cousin, a word.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: All studies here I solemnly defy,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Save how to gall and pinch this Bolingbroke.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And that same sword-and-buckler Prince of Wales—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But that I think his father loves him not

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And would be glad he met with some mischance—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I would have him poisoned with a pot of ale.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Farewell, kinsman. I’ll talk to you

speaker: WORCESTER
text: When you are better tempered to attend.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Why, what a wasp-stung and impatient fool

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Art thou to break into this woman’s mood,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Tying thine ear to no tongue but thine own!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, look you, I am whipped and scourged with rods,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nettled and stung with pismires, when I hear

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of this vile politician, Bolingbroke.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In Richard’s time—what do you call the place?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A plague upon it! It is in Gloucestershire.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: ’Twas where the madcap duke his uncle kept,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: His uncle York, where I first bowed my knee

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Unto this king of smiles, this Bolingbroke.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: ’Sblood, when you and he came back from Ravenspurgh.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: At Berkeley Castle.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: You say true.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, what a candy deal of courtesy

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This fawning greyhound then did proffer me:

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: “Look when his infant fortune came to age,”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And “gentle Harry Percy,” and “kind cousin.”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, the devil take such cozeners!—God forgive me!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Good uncle, tell your tale. I have done.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Nay, if you have not, to it again.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: We will stay your leisure.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I have done, i’ faith.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Then once more to your Scottish prisoners:

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Deliver them up without their ransom straight,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And make the Douglas’ son your only mean

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For powers in Scotland, which, for divers reasons

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Which I shall send you written, be assured

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Will easily be granted.—You, my lord,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Your son in Scotland being thus employed,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Shall secretly into the bosom creep

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Of that same noble prelate well beloved,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The Archbishop.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of York, is it not?

speaker: WORCESTER
text: True, who bears hard

speaker: WORCESTER
text: His brother’s death at Bristol, the Lord Scroop.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I speak not this in estimation,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As what I think might be, but what I know

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Is ruminated, plotted, and set down,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And only stays but to behold the face

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Of that occasion that shall bring it on.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I smell it. Upon my life it will do well.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Before the game is afoot thou still let’st slip.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, it cannot choose but be a noble plot.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And then the power of Scotland and of York

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To join with Mortimer, ha?

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And so they shall.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In faith, it is exceedingly well aimed.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And ’tis no little reason bids us speed

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To save our heads by raising of a head,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For bear ourselves as even as we can,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The King will always think him in our debt,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And think we think ourselves unsatisfied,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Till he hath found a time to pay us home.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And see already how he doth begin

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To make us strangers to his looks of love.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He does, he does. We’ll be revenged on him.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Cousin, farewell. No further go in this

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Than I by letters shall direct your course.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: When time is ripe, which will be suddenly,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I’ll steal to Glendower and Lord Mortimer,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Where you and Douglas and our powers at once,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: As I will fashion it, shall happily meet

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To bear our fortunes in our own strong arms,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Which now we hold at much uncertainty.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Farewell, good brother. We shall thrive, I trust.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Uncle, adieu. O, let the hours be short

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Till fields and blows and groans applaud our sport.
